```{r}
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
# ml_mantel_batch_exact.R
# Batch: por cada pareja (subdir) que contenga dos archivos *_all_aligned.fasta
# hace EXACTAMENTE lo que hace tu ml_and_mantel.R: ML (pml->optim.pml), bootstrap opcional,
# patristic matrices, Mantel (MANTEL_PERM), mirror-tree + perm (MIRROR_PERM).
#
# Ajusta OUT_DIR, MODEL, DO_BOOTSTRAP, BS_REPS, MANTEL_PERM, MIRROR_PERM arriba si quieres.

library(phangorn)
library(ape)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------- USER -----------------
OUT_DIR <- "/home/nacho/HDD16/Nacho/RepliCOOC/rep_distances/letsshowmultirepps/treesreplicons"
# ML options (match your single-pair script)
MODEL <- "GTR"
OPT_GAMMA <- TRUE
OPT_INV <- FALSE
# Bootstrapping (set TRUE to run)
DO_BOOTSTRAP <- TRUE
BS_REPS <- 100

# Permutations
MANTEL_PERM <- 9999
MIRROR_PERM <- 2000

# ----------------------------------------

dirs <- list.dirs(OUT_DIR, recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)
results <- data.frame(pair = character(), repA = character(), repB = character(),
                      n_taxa = integer(), mantel_r = numeric(), mantel_p = numeric(),
                      mirror_r = numeric(), mirror_p_emp = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

for (d in dirs) {
  # identificar archivos alineados *_all_aligned.fasta
  aligned <- list.files(d, pattern = "_all_aligned\\.f(ast)?a$", full.names = TRUE)
  if (length(aligned) < 2) next
  aligned <- sort(aligned)
  alnA <- aligned[1]
  alnB <- aligned[2]
  pair_label <- basename(d)
  OUT_PREFIX <- file.path(d, paste0(pair_label))
  cat("=== Processing pair:", pair_label, "\n")
  cat("  alnA:", alnA, "\n  alnB:", alnB, "\n")

  # -------- read alignments -------
  aln1 <- tryCatch(read.phyDat(alnA, format = "fasta", type = "DNA"),
                   error = function(e){ cat("Error reading", alnA, ":", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
  aln2 <- tryCatch(read.phyDat(alnB, format = "fasta", type = "DNA"),
                   error = function(e){ cat("Error reading", alnB, ":", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
  if (is.null(aln1) || is.null(aln2)) { cat("  Skipping pair due to read error\n"); next }

  # normalize names (accession before '|')
  rename_acc <- function(namesv) {
    sapply(namesv, function(n) if (grepl("\\|", n)) sub("\\|.*$", "", n) else n, USE.NAMES = FALSE)
  }
  acc1 <- rename_acc(names(aln1)); acc2 <- rename_acc(names(aln2))
  # keep first occurrence if duplicates
  if (any(duplicated(acc1))) {
    cat("  Warning: duplicated accessions in aln1; keeping first occurrence.\n")
    keep_idx <- !duplicated(acc1)
    aln1 <- aln1[keep_idx]; acc1 <- acc1[keep_idx]
  }
  if (any(duplicated(acc2))) {
    cat("  Warning: duplicated accessions in aln2; keeping first occurrence.\n")
    keep_idx <- !duplicated(acc2)
    aln2 <- aln2[keep_idx]; acc2 <- acc2[keep_idx]
  }
  names(aln1) <- acc1; names(aln2) <- acc2
  cat("  n tips normalized aln1:", length(aln1), " aln2:", length(aln2), "\n")

  # -------- starting trees (NJ on dist.ml) -------
  cat("  Computing starting NJ trees (dist.ml)...\n")
  d1_start <- dist.ml(aln1)
  nj1 <- NJ(d1_start)
  d2_start <- dist.ml(aln2)
  nj2 <- NJ(d2_start)

  # -------- pml and optim.pml (ML optimization) -------
  cat("  Building pml objects and running optim.pml (trace=1)...\n")
  pml1 <- pml(nj1, data = aln1)
  pml2 <- pml(nj2, data = aln2)
  control <- pml.control(trace = 1)   # same as your script (shows progress)
  fit1 <- tryCatch(optim.pml(pml1, model = MODEL, optGamma = OPT_GAMMA, optInv = OPT_INV, control = control),
                   error = function(e){ cat("  optim.pml failed for aln1:", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
  fit2 <- tryCatch(optim.pml(pml2, model = MODEL, optGamma = OPT_GAMMA, optInv = OPT_INV, control = control),
                   error = function(e){ cat("  optim.pml failed for aln2:", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
  if (is.null(fit1) || is.null(fit2)) { cat("  Skipping pair due to optim.pml failure\n"); next }

  tree1 <- fit1$tree
  tree2 <- fit2$tree

  # save ML trees
  write.tree(tree1, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_IncFII_ML.tree"))
  write.tree(tree2, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_rep_ML.tree"))
  cat("  Saved ML trees.\n")

  # -------- bootstrap if requested -------
  if (DO_BOOTSTRAP) {
    cat("  Running bootstrap (", BS_REPS, "reps) -- may be slow...\n")
    bs1 <- tryCatch(bootstrap.pml(fit1, bs = BS_REPS, optNni = TRUE, control = pml.control(trace = 0)),
                    error = function(e){ cat("  bootstrap failed for aln1:", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
    if (!is.null(bs1)) {
      tree1_bs <- plotBS(tree1, bs1, type = "none")
      write.tree(tree1_bs, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_IncFII_ML_bootstrapped.tree"))
    }
    bs2 <- tryCatch(bootstrap.pml(fit2, bs = BS_REPS, optNni = TRUE, control = pml.control(trace = 0)),
                    error = function(e){ cat("  bootstrap failed for aln2:", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
    if (!is.null(bs2)) {
      tree2_bs <- plotBS(tree2, bs2, type = "none")
      write.tree(tree2_bs, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_rep_ML_bootstrapped.tree"))
    }
    cat("  Bootstrap step finished (if no errors).\n")
  }

  # -------- patristic distances -------
  cat("  Computing patristic distance matrices...\n")
  D1 <- as.matrix(cophenetic.phylo(tree1)); diag(D1) <- 0
  D2 <- as.matrix(cophenetic.phylo(tree2)); diag(D2) <- 0
  write.table(D1, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_IncFII_patristic.tsv"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)
  write.table(D2, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_rep_patristic.tsv"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)

  # -------- intersection and Mantel -------
  common <- intersect(rownames(D1), rownames(D2))
  cat("  Common accessions:", length(common), "\n")
  if (length(common) < 4) { cat("  <4 common taxa -> skipping Mantel\n"); next }
  D1c <- D1[common, common]; D2c <- D2[common, common]

  cat("  Running Mantel (pearson) with", MANTEL_PERM, "permutations...\n")
  set.seed(42)
  mant <- mantel(as.dist(D1c), as.dist(D2c), method = "pearson", permutations = MANTEL_PERM)
  print(mant)
  saveRDS(mant, file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_mantel.rds"))
  write.table(as.data.frame(t(as.numeric(mant[c("statistic","signif")]))),
              file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_mantel_summary.txt"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE,
              col.names = c("Mantel_r","p_value"), row.names = FALSE)

  # -------- mirror-tree correlation + empirical permutations -------
  v1 <- as.vector(as.dist(D1c)); v2 <- as.vector(as.dist(D2c))
  dfm <- data.frame(d1 = v1, d2 = v2)
  cor_test <- cor.test(dfm$d1, dfm$d2, method = "pearson")
  print(cor_test)
  write.table(data.frame(stat = cor_test$statistic, p.value = cor_test$p.value),
              file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_mirror_cor_test.txt"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)

  png(paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_mirror_scatter.png"), width = 1200, height = 1000, res = 150)
  plot(dfm$d1, dfm$d2, xlab = "Patristic distances (repA)", ylab = "Patristic distances (repB)",
       main = paste0(pair_label, " mirror r = ", round(cor(dfm$d1, dfm$d2), 3)))
  abline(lm(dfm$d2 ~ dfm$d1), col = "blue")
  dev.off()

  cat("  Running empirical permutation for mirror-tree (", MIRROR_PERM, "reps)...\n")
  set.seed(42)
  orig_cor <- cor(dfm$d1, dfm$d2)
  perm_cor <- numeric(MIRROR_PERM)
  labels <- rownames(D1c)
  for (i in seq_len(MIRROR_PERM)) {
    perm <- sample(labels)
    D2perm <- D2c[perm, perm]
    perm_cor[i] <- cor(as.vector(as.dist(D1c)), as.vector(as.dist(D2perm)))
  }
  p_emp <- (sum(abs(perm_cor) >= abs(orig_cor)) + 1) / (MIRROR_PERM + 1)
  cat("  Empirical p-value (mirror-tree):", p_emp, "observed cor:", orig_cor, "\n")
  write.table(data.frame(emp_p = p_emp, orig_cor = orig_cor),
              file = paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_mirror_perm_result.txt"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)

  png(paste0(OUT_PREFIX, "_perm_hist.png"), width = 1200, height = 800, res = 150)
  hist(perm_cor, breaks = 60, main = paste0("Permutation null (n=", MIRROR_PERM, ") - observed r=", round(orig_cor, 3)))
  abline(v = orig_cor, col = "red", lwd = 2)
  dev.off()

  # append a summary row to results table
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(pair = pair_label, repA = basename(alnA), repB = basename(alnB),
                                       n_taxa = length(common), mantel_r = mant$statistic, mantel_p = mant$signif,
                                       mirror_r = orig_cor, mirror_p_emp = p_emp, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  cat("=== Done pair:", pair_label, "\n\n")
}

# write combined results
out_table <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "pairwise_mantel_results.tsv")
write.table(results, file = out_table, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
cat("Wrote combined results to", out_table, "\n")

```


```{r}

library(ape)
library(phytools)

pair_dir <- "/home/nacho/HDD16/Nacho/RepliCOOC/rep_distances/letsshowmultirepps/treesreplicons/rep_cluster_252__rep_cluster_735"

treeA <- read.tree(file.path(pair_dir, "rep_cluster_252__rep_cluster_735_IncFII_ML.tree"))
treeB <- read.tree(file.path(pair_dir, "rep_cluster_252__rep_cluster_735_rep_ML.tree"))

# Alineación de tips
common <- intersect(treeA$tip.label, treeB$tip.label)
treeA <- keep.tip(treeA, common)
treeB <- keep.tip(treeB, common)

# Midpoint root
treeA <- midpoint.root(treeA)
treeB <- midpoint.root(treeB)

# Cophylo layout
obj <- cophylo(treeA, treeB, rotate = TRUE)

# -------- EXPORT SVG --------
svg(
  filename = file.path(pair_dir, "tanglegram_rep_cluster_252__rep_cluster_735.svg"),
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)

plot(
  obj,
  link.type = "curved",
  fsize = 0.7,
  lwd = 2,
  col = "black",
  link.lwd = 1,
  link.col = "grey40"
)
title("Tanglegram de replicones — midpoint rooted")

dev.off()

```


```{r}
library(ape)
library(phytools)

pair_dir <- "/home/nacho/HDD16/Nacho/RepliCOOC/rep_distances/letsshowmultirepps/treesreplicons/ColRNAI_rep_cluster_1987__rep_cluster_2358"

treeA <- read.tree(file.path(pair_dir, "ColRNAI_rep_cluster_1987__rep_cluster_2358_IncFII_ML.tree"))
treeB <- read.tree(file.path(pair_dir, "ColRNAI_rep_cluster_1987__rep_cluster_2358_rep_ML.tree"))

# Alineación de tips
common <- intersect(treeA$tip.label, treeB$tip.label)
treeA <- keep.tip(treeA, common)
treeB <- keep.tip(treeB, common)

# Midpoint root
treeA <- midpoint.root(treeA)
treeB <- midpoint.root(treeB)

# Cophylo layout
obj <- cophylo(treeA, treeB, rotate = TRUE)

# -------- EXPORT SVG --------
svg(
  filename = file.path(pair_dir, "tanglegram_ColRNAI_rep_cluster_1987__rep_cluster_2358.svg"),
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)

plot(
  obj,
  link.type = "curved",
  fsize = 0.7,
  lwd = 2,
  col = "black",
  link.lwd = 1,
  link.col = "grey40"
)
title("Tanglegram de replicones — midpoint rooted")

dev.off()



```
